title: Relighting 3D Gaussian Splatting under Near-Field Illumination with Diffusion Priors
teaser: This project models the near-field illumination directly, optimizing an irradiance field from background pixels with a diffusion prior to complete the unobserved regions, for an unbiased material-lighting decomposition and consistent light transport when the object is moved.
abstract: Relighting approaches widely adopt the distant-lighting assumption: the object is treated as isolated in the scene, and all irradiance it receives is assumed to come from infinitely far away, typically represented as an environment map — which is rarely the case in real capture scenarios. Inter-reflection with nearby objects produces shadows, color bleeding, and even mirror reflections, which are then baked into the recovered geometry and materials, resulting in biased estimation and decomposition; avoiding this currently requires either clearing the capture area or explicitly tracing secondary bounces, which is computationally heavy. This project instead models the near-field illumination directly. Since background objects are visible in all captures, foreground and background pixels are first separated: the former supervise the object's geometry and appearance in a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) representation, while the latter are used to optimize an irradiance field describing the near-field lighting around the object. A diffusion prior is introduced to compensate for the incomplete observation and the misalignment in depth estimation of the background. The expected result is improved relighting quality with an unbiased material-lighting decomposition, and plausible light transport effects — consistent shadows and color bleeding — when moving the object within the reconstructed or relit scene.
milestones: Foreground-background separation and joint optimization of the object (3DGS) and a near-field irradiance field from background pixels.
Integrating a diffusion prior to complete the unobserved background regions.
Incorporating the recovered near-field lighting into the material-lighting decomposition; evaluating relighting quality against distant-lighting baselines.
Relocating the object within the reconstructed/relit scene with consistent near-field light transport effects.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: Relightable 3D Gaussians: Realistic Point Cloud Relighting with BRDF Decomposition and Ray Tracing
NeILF++: Inter-Reflectable Light Fields for Geometry and Material Estimation
IR-HGP: Physically-Aware Gaussian Inverse Rendering for High-Illumination Scenes via Generative Priors
